title: Add people management system with person selector and individual expense views

requirements.text: Create a backend data model to store and manage people with fields for id (unique identifier), name (Text), and createdAt (Int timestamp for creation date)

quotes: Add a option for adding people

acceptanceCriteria: Person records are stored in stable storage using Map data structure
Each person has a unique identifier, name, and creation timestamp
Person data persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Implement backend CRUD functions for people: createPerson(name: Text) returns Person, listPeople() returns all people sorted by name, updatePerson(id, name) updates person name, deletePerson(id) removes person and returns success status

quotes: Add a option for adding people

acceptanceCriteria: createPerson validates name is not empty and returns new person record
listPeople returns all people sorted alphabetically by name
updatePerson validates person exists and name is not empty
deletePerson removes person from storage and returns boolean success

requirements.text: Create a People Management component with a list view showing all people and an add person form with name input field, using the same styling patterns as ExpenseForm and ExpenseList components

quotes: People Management Interface: A dedicated section to add, edit, and remove people from your tracker

acceptanceCriteria: Component displays list of all people sorted alphabetically
Inline form allows adding new people with name validation
Each person row includes edit and delete action buttons
Uses shadcn/ui components consistent with existing UI
Integrates React Query hooks for people CRUD operations

requirements.text: Add edit functionality to People Management component allowing inline editing of person names with save and cancel actions
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking edit icon switches person row to edit mode with text input
Save button updates person name and exits edit mode
Cancel button discards changes and exits edit mode
Validation prevents saving empty names

requirements.text: Add delete functionality to People Management component with confirmation dialog before removing a person
acceptanceCriteria: Delete button shows confirmation dialog with person's name
Confirming deletion removes person from backend and updates UI
Canceling dialog keeps person in list

requirements.text: Update ExpenseForm component to replace the free-text person name input with a searchable dropdown selector populated from the managed people list, maintaining the current form layout and validation

quotes: Enhanced Expense Entry: Updated form to select from your list of people

acceptanceCriteria: Person field uses shadcn/ui Select or Combobox component
Dropdown displays all people sorted alphabetically
Selected person name is used when creating/editing expenses
Form validation ensures a person is selected before submission
Component fetches people list using React Query

requirements.text: Add quick-add functionality to ExpenseForm person selector allowing users to create new people directly from the dropdown without leaving the expense entry form

quotes: with option to add new people on-the-fly

acceptanceCriteria: Person selector includes 'Add new person' option or button
Clicking add new shows inline input field for name entry
Saving new person immediately creates it in backend and selects it
New person appears in dropdown and is auto-selected in form

requirements.text: Implement backend query function getPersonExpenses(personId: Text, year: ?Nat, month: ?Nat) that returns all expenses for a specific person, optionally filtered by year and/or month, sorted by date descending

quotes: Individual Person View: A detailed page for each person showing their complete expense history

acceptanceCriteria: Function filters expenses by matching person id
Optional year parameter filters expenses to specified year
Optional month parameter filters expenses to specified month within year
Returns expenses sorted by date in descending order (newest first)
Returns empty array if person has no expenses

requirements.text: Create a PersonDetailView component that displays a specific person's complete expense history in a table format matching ExpenseList styling, showing all expense details (amount, description, date, category) sorted by date

quotes: A detailed page for each person showing their complete expense history, total spending, and daily averages

acceptanceCriteria: Component accepts person id as parameter
Displays person name as page header
Shows table of all person's expenses using same layout as ExpenseList
Expenses are sorted by date descending (newest first)
Includes edit and delete actions for each expense

requirements.text: Add summary statistics section to PersonDetailView showing total spending in ₹ and daily average calculated across all time for that person

quotes: showing their complete expense history, total spending, and daily averages

acceptanceCriteria: Summary section displays above expense table
Shows total amount spent by person formatted as Indian Rupees
Shows daily average calculated as total divided by number of unique days with expenses
Uses same styling and currency formatting as existing summary components

requirements.text: Add a People tab to the main App navigation alongside Expenses and Summary tabs, displaying the People Management component when selected

quotes: A dedicated section to add, edit, and remove people

acceptanceCriteria: New 'People' tab appears in main navigation tabs
Clicking People tab displays People Management component
Tab maintains same styling as existing Expenses and Summary tabs
Active tab state is managed correctly

requirements.text: Add clickable links from person names in ExpenseList and PersonBreakdown components that navigate to the PersonDetailView for that specific person

quotes: A detailed page for each person

acceptanceCriteria: Person names in ExpenseList are rendered as clickable links
Person names in PersonBreakdown table are rendered as clickable links
Clicking person name navigates to PersonDetailView for that person
Links use consistent styling with hover states

requirements.text: Create React Query hooks in useQueries.ts for people management: useCreatePerson, useListPeople, useUpdatePerson, useDeletePerson, and usePersonExpenses following the existing pattern for expense hooks
acceptanceCriteria: All hooks use useActor to get backend actor instance
Mutation hooks (create, update, delete) invalidate relevant queries on success
Query hooks use appropriate cache keys and refetch strategies
Hooks follow same patterns as existing useExpenses hooks

constraints: Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useInternetIdentity.tsx, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui/*
Keep existing expense tracking functionality unchanged
Use existing OKLCH color system and styling patterns
Maintain Indian Rupee (₹) currency formatting throughout
nonGoals: Replacing the existing expense entry form or list view
Changing the backend storage architecture from Map to another structure
Adding bulk import/export functionality for people
Implementing person profile pictures or additional metadata
Creating multi-user or shared expense tracking features
